reasoning: Dan Osborn officially formed an exploratory committee on April 3, 2025, to run against incumbent U.S. Senator Pete Ricketts in the 2026 election. The window from April 3 to the June 1, 2025 deadline is roughly eight weeks. Candidates who form an exploratory committee typically announce their formal candidacy within 1 to 3 months, making June 1 a highly relevant threshold. He had previously expressed a desire to decide his 2026 plans by "early March" before ultimately settling on a Senate exploratory bid in April. The fact that he was already "office shopping" and aiming for early timelines suggests he is actively gearing up for a campaign, but the delay between March and April indicates he might take a deliberate pace. Assuming an 80% likelihood that he ultimately decides to run, and considering the 59-day window (April 3 to June 1) sits squarely in the middle of a typical 30-to-90-day exploratory phase, I estimate there is around a 45% chance he officially crosses the threshold from "exploring" to an announced candidacy before June 1.
